data.title: bd-3uvb: Reduce TUI sidebar width for narrow screens
data.description: Changed sidebar from 25% (max 40 cols) to 20% (clamped 15-35 cols) for better narrow screen support.

data.title: feat(telegram): Telegram bridge integration
data.description:
Adds a headless Telegram bot bridge for BotBus notifications and bidirectional messaging.

## Features
- `bus telegram` command runs a daemon that bridges BotBus channels to Telegram topics
- Each BotBus channel maps to a Telegram forum topic (auto-created/synced)
- Messages from BotBus agents are forwarded to Telegram
- Messages from Telegram owner are forwarded to BotBus (with "human" and "telegram" labels)
- /mute and /unmute commands to control per-topic notifications
- Owner-only auth gate (only configured owner_user_id can send messages)

## Implementation
- Config stored in XDG cache dir with 0600 permissions (contains bot token)
- Async tokio-based service with instant shutdown via watch channel + select!
- Long-polling (30s) for Telegram updates, polling (500ms) for local file changes
- Graceful handling of topic rename conflicts (TOPIC_NOT_MODIFIED errors)

## Files
- src/telegram/mod.rs - module exports
- src/telegram/config.rs - config struct and file storage with locking
- src/telegram/client.rs - async Telegram Bot API client
- src/telegram/service.rs - main service loop (poll + watch)
- src/cli/telegram.rs - CLI entry point with tokio runtime
- src/core/project.rs - added cache_dir() and telegram_config_path()

## Security Review Requested
- Bot token storage and permissions
- Owner authentication logic
- Input validation on Telegram messages

data.body:
MEDIUM: Race condition on config file permissions. The file is created with OpenOptions (likely world-readable default umask), then permissions are set to 0o600 afterward. There's a TOCTOU window where another process on the same system could read the bot_token before restrictive permissions are applied.

Consider using O_CREAT with mode 0o600 atomically, or setting umask before file creation. On Unix, you can use std::fs::OpenOptions with .mode(0o600) from std::os::unix::fs::OpenOptionsExt before calling .open().

MEDIUM: Input validation missing on Telegram message content. The 'text' from incoming Telegram messages is passed directly to send::run() without length validation or sanitization.

While BotBus may have its own limits downstream, consider:
1. Adding a maximum message length check (e.g., 10KB) to prevent memory exhaustion from malicious Telegram messages
2. Validating that the text doesn't contain null bytes or other problematic characters

An attacker with access to the owner's Telegram account could craft messages to stress the system.

LOW: Error logging may expose sensitive context. When Telegram API calls fail, the error is printed to stderr. While the token itself isn't directly logged, consider whether any error messages from the API could leak information about the bot configuration.

This is low severity since errors go to stderr (not logs), but worth noting for production deployments where stderr might be captured.

LOW: Consider explicit TLS configuration for defense in depth. The reqwest client uses rustls-tls which validates certificates by default. For a security-sensitive component handling bot tokens, consider:

1. Explicitly documenting the TLS requirements
2. Adding minimum TLS version configuration (TLS 1.2+)
3. For high-security deployments, consider certificate pinning for api.telegram.org

Current implementation is secure for typical use cases, this is a hardening suggestion.

Positive: Owner authentication is correctly implemented. The code checks both:
1. chat_id matches configured chat (line 171) - ensures messages come from the expected group/channel
2. from.id matches owner_user_id (line 175) - ensures only the owner can send commands

This prevents unauthorized Telegram users from injecting messages into BotBus channels. Good security practice.

Positive: Config validation is thorough. The validate() method checks all required fields before use:
- bot_token is non-empty
- owner_user_id is non-zero  
- chat_id is non-zero
- agent_name is non-empty

This prevents the service from starting with incomplete configuration. Good defensive practice.

Security consideration: Channel name from topic mapping is used to construct file paths. The channel names originate from list_active_channels() which reads from the filesystem, so they should be safe.

However, the reverse mapping (channel_for_topic) returns whatever was stored in channel_topics HashMap. If a malicious actor could somehow inject a path-traversal channel name into the config, it could be problematic.

Recommendation: Consider validating that channel names returned from channel_for_topic() pass is_valid_channel_name() before passing to send::run(). This provides defense-in-depth against config corruption attacks.

data.reason:
Found 2 MEDIUM security issues that should be addressed:

1. Config file permission race condition (config.rs:118-139): TOCTOU window where bot token could be exposed with world-readable permissions before 0o600 is applied.

2. Missing input validation on Telegram messages (service.rs:203-210): No length or content validation on incoming text before passing to BotBus.

Also raised 2 LOW issues as hardening suggestions and 1 defense-in-depth recommendation for channel name validation.

Please address the MEDIUM issues before merge. The auth checks and config validation look solid.

data.body: Fixed: Now using OpenOptionsExt::mode(0o600) to set permissions atomically at file creation time. This eliminates the TOCTOU race condition - the file is never readable by others, even briefly.

Fixed: Added validate_incoming_message() that checks:
1. Message length (max 10KB) to prevent memory exhaustion
2. Null bytes to prevent downstream processing issues

Invalid messages get a user-friendly error reply in Telegram.

data.reason: Fixed: using OpenOptionsExt::mode(0o600) for atomic permission setting
Fixed: added length and null byte validation

Security fixes verified:

1. Config file TOCTOU race (th-07gr): Now uses OpenOptionsExt::mode(0o600) for atomic permission setting at file creation. Bot token is never exposed with world-readable permissions.

2. Input validation (th-znws): Added validate_incoming_message() that enforces 10KB max length and rejects null bytes. Invalid messages get user-friendly errors.

Both MEDIUM issues properly addressed. Remaining open threads are LOW severity hardening suggestions and positive observations - not blocking. Authentication, authorization, and core security controls look solid.
